verdicts.reason: The slide sorter view and its reordering logic have been exhaustively simulated and verified, and no new correctness risks or edge cases were identified.
The Slide Sorter View reuses existing, constrained rendering patterns and does not introduce new attack surfaces or trust boundary violations beyond the established architecture.

[AUTO-DOWNGRADED: goalpost move, 0.40 overlap vs prior ui objection] The slide sorter grid lacks efficient keyboard navigation via arrow keys, forcing users to tab through every slide individually, which is high friction for larger decks.
verdicts.required_fix: Implement roving tabindex for arrow-key navigation within the slide sorter grid, allowing users to move focus between cards using arrow keys.
verdicts.evidence: sorter-card interaction within #sorter-modal; absence of arrow key handling for grid navigation

verdicts.reason: Slide Sorter feature is highly discoverable with clear in-app hints, tooltips, and comprehensive documentation.
The slide sorter view provides a crucial overview and reordering capability essential for efficient presentation creation and navigation, addressing a common need in presentation software.
verdicts.evidence: This feature is a standard component of professional presentation software like PowerPoint, Keynote, and Google Slides, indicating its high utility for users managing and organizing complex presentations.

verdicts.reason: The visual drag-and-drop slide sorter, with instant markdown source updates and content badges, provides a signature interactive experience unique among markdown-first presentation tools.
The deliverable adheres to all documented architectural constraints and prior council adjudications for markdown-deck.
